{{Quote|OOM-14 has allowed the Naboo to make off with a precious droid control program.|Nute Gunray|audio=OOM-14Sucks.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
The '''droid control program''' was a piece of technology used to keep the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] activated, stored in a [[Color/Legends|brown]] crate. Five programs were sent to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] during the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation's]] [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]], where one was stolen from the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] [[OOM-14/Legends|OOM-14]]. It was later [[Capture of Harte Secur|recovered]] by the [[Droid/Legends|droid]] [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]] and reactivated by a [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] [[Unidentified Neimoidian technician (Harte Secur)|technician]].

==Description==
{{Quote|There are five droid control programs down on Naboo with our initial landing force.|Nute Gunray|audio=FivePrograms.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
The [[Droid/Legends|droid]] control program was a technological device that kept the [[Trade Federation Droid Army/Legends|Trade Federation Droid Army]] online.<ref name="GB" /> It was contained inside<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref> a [[Color/Legends|brown]] cubed<ref name="GB" /> crate<ref name="Prima" /> on top of a round base. The cube had indented sections on its faces that had white patterns of a plus sign overlayed on a circle.<ref name="GB" />

==History==
{{Quote|Droid control program recovered.|OOM-9|audio=DroidControlProgramRecovered.flac|<ref name="GB" />}}
[[File:Harte_Secur_Research_Center.png|thumb|left|300px|The droid control program was captured by the Naboo and taken to a research center south of Harte Secur.]]
During the [[Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation's]] [[Invasion of Naboo/Legends|invasion]] of the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], five droid control programs were sent with their invading [[Army/Legends|army]]. One program was placed in the protection of the [[B1-series battle droid/Legends|B1-series battle droid]] [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[OOM-14/Legends|OOM-14]], but it was stolen from him by the [[Royal Naboo Security Forces/Legends|Royal Naboo Security Forces]] team [[Force Command Two]]. The loss of the program rendered OOM-14's forces motionless at a [[Deactivated Camp]], while the program was taken to a [[Research Center]] south of the Naboo [[City/Legends|city]] [[Harte Secur]] for analysis. [[Viceroy of the Trade Federation/Legends|Trade Federation Viceroy]] [[Nute Gunray/Legends|Nute Gunray]] was concerned that the [[Naboo (people)/Legends|Naboo]] would be able to duplicate the program and turn the army against the Trade Federation.<ref name="GB" />

The Federation also jammed Naboo communications around Harte Secur to prevent [[Slicer/Legends|hackers]] from duplicating and spreading a rogue version of the program. Gunray charged his most trusted [[OOM command battle droid/Legends|battle droid]], [[OOM-9/Legends|OOM-9]], with retrieving the program, providing a [[Neimoidian/Legends|Neimoidian]] [[Unidentified Neimoidian technician (Harte Secur)|technician]] and a small task force of droids to complete the objective. The droids [[Capture of Harte Secur|stormed]] the research center and [[Death/Legends|killed]] the royal [[Trooper/Legends|troopers]] there, afterward collecting the program inside OOM-9's personal [[Armored Assault Tank/Legends|Heavy Armored Assault Tank]], which the [[Technician/Legends|technician]] had occupied. The droids returned to the camp and the technician used the program to reactivate OOM-14's army, and they went on to destroy Harte Secur.<ref name="Prima" /> Gunray reported the recovery of the program to his secret benefactor, the [[Dark Lord of the Sith/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Darth Sidious]].<ref name="GB" />

==Behind the scenes==
The droid control program was first indirectly mentioned in ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]'' by [[Steven L. Kent]],<ref name="Prima" /> released on [[November 7]], [[2001]].<ref name="PrimaDate">{{Amazon|url=Star-Wars-Galactic-Battlegrounds-Official/dp/0761537503|text=Star Wars Galactic Battlegrounds: Prima's Official Strategy Guide}}</ref> It appeared and was identified in the [[LucasArts]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds]]'',<ref name="GB" /> released on [[November 11]] the same year.<ref name="GBDate">{{GameSpot|url=games/star-wars-galactic-battlegrounds|text=Star Wars: Galactic Battlegrounds}}</ref> The program is the central focus of the second level of the Trade Federation campaign, "Behind the Lines."<ref name="GB" />

The strategy guide compares the functionality of the program with a [[Holocron/Legends|holocron]], in that only a specific unit can utilize it, that being the Neimoidian technician instead of a [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] or [[Sith/Legends|Sith]]. The strategy guide recommends that the player keep the technician inside OOM-9's AAT, allowing the program to automatically be collected.<ref name="Prima" /> If the technician is not inside the AAT, moving him near the program causes it to disappear, only reappearing when the technician returns to the camp. If the player places the program in a custom scenario, it can be picked up by Jedi or Sith units, which turns the program into a holocron.<ref name="GB" />
